summary: Re-tested after auth migration to httpOnly cookies. Backend: 21/21 pytest suite PASSES with cookie-based auth_headers fixture (login sets access_token httpOnly cookie; /auth/me and all protected endpoints return 401 without cookie and 200 with it; logout clears cookie). Frontend: all admin flows work — login redirects to /admin, cookie is httpOnly, session persists across full page reload, Jobs create/edit/copy-link/delete, Site Content save+persists across reload, Check-Ins table renders, logout clears session and /admin redirects to /admin/login. Public check-in flow: form fill -> Share Location -> GDPR privacy modal -> consent -> geolocation (mocked lat 40.7128 lng -74.006) -> success + pin on Leaflet map + pin-count=1. One minor testability issue found: data-testid="leaflet-map" passed to react-leaflet MapContainer is not forwarded to the DOM (0 elements). The map itself is rendered as .leaflet-container and functions correctly, but tests that key off leaflet-map cannot find it.

testability_issues.issue: data-testid="leaflet-map" is passed as a prop to <MapContainer> but react-leaflet does NOT forward unknown props to the underlying div, so the attribute never appears in the DOM. .leaflet-container is present (1) and the map renders fine, but selectors on [data-testid="leaflet-map"] find 0 elements.
testability_issues.fix: Wrap MapContainer in a plain <div data-testid="leaflet-map"> or add the attr via ref/whenReady onto the container element.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Wrap the react-leaflet <MapContainer> in a <div data-testid="leaflet-map"> in /app/frontend/src/components/MapView.js so automated tests can locate the map by its documented testid.
(From iteration_1, still open, low priority) Wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch in AdminDashboard.copyLink for graceful degradation on clipboard permission denial.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _set_auth_cookie: httponly=True, secure=True, samesite='none', max_age=43200, path='/' — matches httpOnly-cookie playbook. Requires same-site or explicit CORS_ORIGINS+allow_credentials=True (both present).
server.py get_current_user: correctly checks Authorization header first then falls back to access_token cookie — good backward-compat.
AuthContext.js: correctly removed localStorage; calls /auth/me on mount to restore session; login/logout hit the API. Cookie carries the session — verified httpOnly=True in browser.
api.js: withCredentials:true and no token interceptor — clean.
seed_admin logic: updates password hash on existing admin if env password changed (verify_password fallback) — matches playbook idempotency requirement.
bcrypt: uses bcrypt.hashpw with gensalt() -> $2b$ prefix; verified by successful login round-trip.
No brute-force lockout implemented on /api/auth/login (5-fail threshold from playbook not enforced). Not part of this review's feature list but flagging for completeness.

AdminDashboard.js still 350+ lines with 3 sub-tabs inline (CheckInsTab, JobsTab, SettingsTab) plus JobDialog — consider splitting into /components/admin/ for maintainability (non-blocking, carried over from iteration_1).
MapView.js: data-testid on MapContainer not forwarded — testability nit.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all requested flows (auth migration, admin CRUD, settings persist, public check-in with GDPR + geolocation, logout)

seed_data_creation: None permanent. Test job TEST_UI2_Job_edited was created and deleted during Playwright. One leftover TEST_UI_Job_1 from an initial retry cleaned up post-test via API. TEST_John check-ins from pytest were cascade-deleted with their parent test jobs. AUTOTEST_Job Renamed remains from iteration_1 seed data (harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth is fully cookie-based (httpOnly access_token). Pytest fixture `auth_headers` in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py sends `Cookie: access_token=...` and works. For Playwright: page.context.grant_permissions(["geolocation"], origin=<REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL>) + page.context.set_geolocation({lat:40.7128,lng:-74.006}) before triggering share-location. Use longer waits (~1s) for the settings tab load after mount, otherwise input_value returns empty. The [data-testid="leaflet-map"] selector currently does NOT resolve — use `.leaflet-container` instead until MapView.js is fixed.
rca_of_the_issue:
leaflet-map RCA: — react-leaflet's MapContainer explicitly picks known props (whenCreated, center, zoom, style, className, id, placeholder, etc.) and does NOT spread arbitrary attributes onto the rendered
. So the data-testid is silently dropped. Fix:
.
